title: Tamil Nadu Assembly poll: Congress names 27 candidates; Melur nominee yet to be announced
content: New entrant G.K.M. Tamil Kumaran, son of former Pattali Makkal Katchi (PMK) president G.K. Mani, has been given the Pennagaram seat despite severe opposition from some senior leaders

title: Assembly Elections LIVE: Trinamool seeks removal of Bhabanipur RO, alleges proximity with BJP's Suvendu Adhikari
content: Yesterday, West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee blamed AIMIM and ISF for the gherao of judicial officers in Malda district and accused the Congress and the BJP of instigation.

title: Karnataka tops in women self-help groups getting training as drone pilots under ‘Namo Drone Didi Yojana’
content: In Karnataka, Koppal district topped the list, with 13 members of SHGs followed by Kalaburagi (12) and Mandya (11)

title: NIMHANS review flags enduring impact of child suicides on parents, mental health professionals
content: The study finds that parents experience intense and prolonged grief marked by guilt, self-blame, and a persistent search for answers, while mental health professionals experience a parallel emotional burden

title: Kerala polls: CPI(M) doesn’t need to launch all leaders as MLAs or for other positions for life, says party veteran M.M. Mani
content: M.M. Mani predicts a resounding third consecutive term for the LDF in the Assembly with over 100 seats

title: Rajnath Singh lays foundation stone of a Large Cavitation Tunnel at NSTL, Visakhapatnam
content: Once operational, it will provide precise validation for the hydrodynamic designs of major naval platforms, such as destroyers and aircraft carriers.

title: Shashi Tharoor’s gunman, driver attacked in Kerala’s Malappuram; one held
content: As per FIR, the incident occurred around 7.30 pm on Friday when there was a roadblock at a bridge as Tharoor was on his way to attend an election campaign event of a Congress leader

title: Kerala elections: AICC moves ECI against CPI(M) channel over ‘defaming’ K.C. Venugopal
content: AICC legal cell chairperson Abhisekh Singhvi filed a petition seeking the ECI’s urgent intervention. The petition noted that the fabrication and subsequent dissemination of the picture on social media, including CPI(M) handles, constituted a violation of the model code of conduct.

title: Iran-Israel conflict: Kin of Indian seafarer killed in strike off Oman move Bombay HC, seek return of remains
content: Family alleges delay in bringing back mortal remains of crew member who died after missile strike off Oman coast

title: Maharashtra reshuffles 25 IAS officers; key cities get new Collectors, Commissioners
content: In Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Municipal Commissioner G. Shrikant has been moved to Navi Mumbai, where he will serve as Chief Executive Officer of the Jal Jeevan Mission

title: CBSE’s recent curriculum not academic reform but calculated attempt at linguistic imposition: Stalin
content: Stalin said under the guise of promoting “Indian languages,” the BJP-led NDA government is aggressively advancing a centralising agenda that privileges Hindi while systematically marginalising India’s rich and diverse linguistic heritage

title: Bengal polls: TMC seeks removal of Bhabanipur RO, alleges proximity with BJP's Suvendu
content: According to the complaint, the RO had earlier served as block development officer in Nandigram-II, where his proximity to Mr. Adhikari was allegedly visible in public engagements

title: Muslim youth beaten up, high drama before police station over allegations of ‘love jihad’ in Hubballi
content: The youth’s family members claimed that the couple were in a relationship for the last three years and her parents were aware of the matter

title: Raghav Chadha is ‘compromised’, says Bhagwant Mann
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann says,”If any individual does not support these collective decisions and goes against the party line, then it is against the party whip. If anyone goes against the party whip, action will be taken.”

title: Sunetra Pawar to file nomination on April 6, contest won’t be unopposed in Baramati
content: The bypolls are slated to test MVA’s unity even as the Mahayuti will rally behind Sunetra Pawar for the Baramati bypolls; Ms. Pawar, who is the DCM at present, will have to get elected in either House of the legislature within six months of assuming office as the DCM

title: Nine of family killed as car falls into well in Nashik district
content: The victims were heading home after attending a function at a banquet hall in the area when their car fell into a well near the venue

title: Cultures converge as Andhra University marks its centenary
content: The celebrations have become a platform for international students to showcase their cultures, traditions, languages, and cuisines. The festivities highlight the university’s long-standing international relations, reaffirming its role as a hub where local heritage meets global exchange. In the lead-up to its Foundation Day on April 26, 2026, the university has been organising a series of programmes to celebrate its academic and cultural heritage.

title: Congress MLA disqualified from M.P. Assembly hours after sentence; Congress slams ‘hasty’ move
content: The Assembly Secretariat disqualified Mr. Bharti’s legislative membership and declared Datia constituency as vacant

title: Jan Vishwas Bill: Commerce Ministry wants departments to consider withdrawing cases involving minor offences
content: Piyush Goyal said as per estimates, there are five crore pending court cases relating to minor offences, majority of which should have never gone to the courts

title: Central nod for grant for establishing EV chargers in Kerala
content: The grant has been approved by the Union Ministry of Heavy Industries on the basis of proposals filed by the Kerala State Electricity Board (KSEB), which is the State-level nodal agency for the PM E-DRIVE scheme

title: Indian Navy asserts Arabian Sea dominance during Operation Sindoor
content: INS Vikrant-led carrier battle group and forward deployment near Karachi kept Pakistan on the defensive

title: In poll-bound Assam, Adityanath stresses U.P. model of dealing with rioters
content: The Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister says drew a parallel between ‘rioters’ in his home State and ‘infiltrators’ in Assam

title: HC permits private colleges to collect fees from students under fee reimbursement scheme
content: Describing the order ‘as an interim measure’, the judge made it clear that the private colleges should refund the tuition fees to the respective students if the writ petitions filed by them were dismissed during the final adjudication.

title: Multiple security agencies target Kashmir employees to create an atmosphere of fear: Mirwaiz
content: “Kashmir was recording daily filing of cases, chargesheets, and arrests of Kashmiris by the multiple agencies operating like the SIA, Counter Intelligence Kashmir, Cyber Cell, Anti-Corruption Bureau and National Investigation Agency,” says Mr. Farooq

title: Pednekar Withdraws Proposal to Include Manache Shlok in BMC Schools After MIM Opposition
content: Uddhav Sena leader faces criticism after drawing a connection between the 17th-century text and Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar’s formative years

title: NSUI workers clash with police over RSS-linked event in Rajasthan University
content: The student wing of the Opposition Congress took exception to a programme being organised by Marudhara Nari Sashaktikaran Sangathan at the humanities auditorium on the university campus, saying it was meant to promote the RSS ideology on the pretext of deliberating on women’s empowerment

title: ‘On Kharat’s call list, DCM Eknath Shinde, Minister Shirsat, Uddhav’s close aide Milind Narvekar’
content: The maximum number of calls was between NCP leader Rupali Chakankar and Ashok Kharat. There were 177 calls between the duo. Ms Chakankar’s sister too spoke with Kharat 236 times

title: Congress MLA disqualified from Assembly hours after sentence; Congress slams ‘hasty’ move
content: In a notification issued, the Assembly Secretariat disqualified Mr. Bharti’s legislative membership and declared Datia constituency as vacant

title: B.P. Yatnal, expelled BJP leader, begins campaign for BJP nominee in Bagalkot
content: He did not criticise Umesh Meti, the Congress candidate; instead, he trained his guns on Chief Minister Siddaramaiah

title: POK will reintegrate into India soon: All India Imam Organisation chief Umer Ahmed Ilyasi
content: “Standing in Kashmir, I want to convey to our neighbouring country that POK, an integral part of India, will witness ‘ghar wapsi’ and rejoin India. People of POK want to have a referendum there after witnessing the current development pace in Kashmir,” says Mr. Ilyasi

title: Fadnavis demands SIR in Maharashtra, says problems with electoral rolls
content: ”We want SIR (Special Intensive Revision) in Maharashtra. There are problems with the electoral rolls, and we have even been raising them. But while the Opposition says there are problems, it opposes SIR,” Fadnavis said

title: Shiv Sena (UBT) open to alliance with MNS for Maharashtra’s benefit, says Sanjay Raut
content: There is no political ego in it, says the Rajya Sabha MP, as speculation grows about a reconciliation between Uddhav and Raj Thackeray

title: U.P. ATS arrests four members of ‘terror network’ linked with Pakistani handlers
content: The Uttar Pradesh ATS received intelligence that a group was in constant contact with a Pakistani handler through various social media platforms and was doing anti-national activities, says official statement

title: Great Nicobar Project: Draft plan to ‘relocate’ affected Nicobarese families creates confusion
content: Absence of clarity on relocation sites, numbers, consent process raises concern among tribal communities.

title: Pakistan jibes become central to BJP’s Assam poll narrative
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has been attacking Congress leader Gaurav Gogoi over his alleged Pakistan connections, his latest barb was aimed at the mother of a candidate whom he accused of sympathising with ‘anti-national’ elements

title: INS Aridhaman, nuclear-powered submarine, enters India’s naval fleet
content: As per sources in the Navy, the commissioning was not made public and was carried out quietly, but Mr. Singh dropped enough hints to acknowledge the commissioning of the vessel

title: Rajasthan High Court expunges remarks on Transgender Act
content: Expunged sections were “included by mistake” in a March 30 judgment issued hours before the Act got Presidential assent, says Bench; includes statement that trans rights must not be “rendered illusory by procedural constraints”

title: Compliance eased for managing plastic waste
content: New provisions allow companies to carry forward the shortfall in meeting targets and formalise a system of tradable certificates

title: Union Home Ministry revises classification of districts affected by left-wing extremism
content: Red Corridor shrinks to just two districts in 2026, reflecting a shift in the severity of left-wing extremism across affected regions

title: Chinese CCTV camera ban shakes Hyderabad trade; Indian brand prices surge, stock piles up, sales drop
content: Demand shifts to Indian CCTV brands, discussions on replacing Chinese systems begin across government and large projects

title: 7th India-flagged LPG tanker crosses Hormuz, 17 in queue
content: In a significant maritime achievement, an Indian LPG tanker has successfully maneuvered through the strategic Strait of Hormuz, flanked by six compatriots that have already reached Indian shores. However, the tension persists as seventeen Indian vessels, two of which are LPG carriers, hold position on the western side, biding their time for a chance to make the journey.

title: Virat Kohli’s cheeky response to ‘overseas player’ taunts shuts down London trolls
content: Virat Kohli has made an immediate impact in the IPL, leading Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a season-opening victory. Despite a break and time spent in London with family, Kohli's performance remains strong. He humorously addressed social media jokes about his overseas status, emphasizing his commitment to the sport and the validation that comes with hard-earned success.

title: After being labelled as 'terrorist' company by Iran, Dell tells employees not to travel
content: Dell Technologies has halted employee travel to the Middle East until mid-April following threats from Iran's IRGC, which named the company as a potential target. The tech giant is prioritizing staff safety amid escalating regional tensions. Employees in affected areas are instructed to work from home, and transit through these regions is also restricted.

title: Iran downs F-15, A-10 jets: First US combat aircraft losses in over 20 years
content: Iran has shot down two US military aircraft, an F-15E Strike Eagle and an A-10 attack aircraft, marking the first such incidents in over two decades. Despite US claims of Iran's weakened capabilities, these events highlight Tehran's continued ability to retaliate, possibly with shoulder-fired missiles, even as US forces conduct extensive operations.

title: 'One by one': Another US jet shot down by Iran, crashes into Hormuz; watch
content: An American A-10 Thunderbolt II jet crashed into the Strait of Hormuz shortly after an F-15E Strike Eagle was reportedly shot down over Iran. The Iranian embassy in South Africa shared footage, claiming "American fighter jets are being shot down one by one." The A-10's pilot was safely rescued, though details surrounding the crash remain scarce.

title: Tharoor's convoy blocked in Kerala's Wandoor, five men assault his gunman, driver
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor's convoy faced an alleged attack in Kerala's Wandoor on Friday evening.  Two vehicles reportedly blocked the road, leading to an assault on Tharoor's gunman and driver when they attempted to clear the obstruction. Police have arrested one of the five identified accused and are pursuing the others.

title: Reason the 6am email from 'Oracle leadership' told thousands of laid-off employees
content: Oracle has begun its largest-ever round of layoffs, with employees receiving termination emails from "Oracle Leadership" at 6AM saying their roles had been eliminated after "careful consideration of Oracle's current business needs." Around 10,000 employees have reportedly been cut so far, with estimates suggesting the total could reach 30,000. The email gave employees a narrow window to act before systems were deactivated.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ crosses Rs 1,500 crore mark worldwide in 16 days
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' continues its impressive box office run, crossing Rs 1500 crore worldwide. The spy thriller saw a significant jump on its third Friday, with India net collections nearing Rs 1000 crore. The film's strong performance indicates further growth over the weekend.

title: Virat and Anushka’s Mumbai home: All about the power couple's stylish abode
content: In the vibrant city of Mumbai, cricketer Virat Kohli and actress Anushka Sharma have settled into a breathtaking apartment that commands a staggering value of Rs 34 crore. Perched high on the 35th floor, this opulent residence spans 7,171 sq ft and boasts four bedrooms, all while providing mesmerizing views of the Arabian Sea.

title: Ruturaj Gaikwad ‘threatened’ by Sanju Samson at CSK? Ex-captain’s big claim
content: Michael Vaughan and Simon Doull question Chennai Super Kings' captaincy dynamics, suggesting Ruturaj Gaikwad might feel threatened by new signing Sanju Samson's leadership credentials.  Despite Samson's underwhelming start, his past success as captain at Rajasthan Royals looms large. This discussion follows CSK's second consecutive defeat, highlighting evolving leadership concerns within the team.

title: As Apple turns 50, a look back the 'most important call' Steve Jobs made to Bill Gates
content: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates reflected on the pivotal 1997 moment when Microsoft invested in a struggling Apple, preventing its bankruptcy. Gates expressed no regrets about the intervention, acknowledging Apple's subsequent crucial role in the mobile era. He did, however, lament selling Microsoft's 5% stake in Apple, calling it a foolish decision.

title: ‘I decided not to contest’: Annamalai breaks silence on ‘denied ticket’ speculation
content: Former Tamil Nadu BJP chief K. Annamalai has voluntarily opted out of contesting the upcoming assembly elections, stating he informed the party leadership in writing. Union minister Piyush Goyal confirmed Annamalai will focus on statewide campaigning.

title: 'Scared of PM Modi?': AAP corners Raghav Chadha
content: Internal rifts within the Aam Aadmi Party have surfaced publicly after Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha was removed as deputy leader. Senior leaders, including Atishi and Bhagwant Mann, have sharply criticized Chadha, accusing him of being scared of Prime Minister Modi and deviating from party lines on crucial national issues.

title: Shreyas Iyer in trouble: PBKS captain fined, one step away from ban
content: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer faces a hefty ₹24 lakh fine for a slow over-rate, marking their second such offence. The team's playing XI also incurred fines. Another breach could lead to Iyer's suspension and a ₹30 lakh penalty, adding pressure as PBKS aims for consistency with many matches remaining.

title: 9 members of a family, including 6 children, dead as car falls into water-filled well
content: A tragic accident in Dindori, Nashik, claimed the lives of nine family members. The group, including six children, was returning from a coaching class get-together when their vehicle veered off the road. It plunged into a deep well in Shivaji Nagar late Friday night. Rescue operations were swift but sadly unsuccessful.

title: 'Can anyone find our pilots? Please?': Iran ridicules US following American jet loss
content: Iran's Parliament Speaker mocked the US after an F-15E fighter jet crashed over Iran, questioning the Pentagon's claims of air superiority. The fate of the two-member crew is unknown, with US teams reportedly scrambling for a rescue. This incident follows a series of US aircraft losses amid escalating regional tensions and threats against Iran.

title: Petrol shock, free rides & more: How is Pakistan dealing with Hormuz energy crisis
content: Pakistan grapples with soaring fuel prices, prompting free public transport in Islamabad and Punjab for 30 days.  Despite a revised petrol price, public anger persists.  The government aims to ease economic burdens amid global energy disruptions and IMF pressure, with similar measures seen in Karachi and Bangladesh.

title: 'Our home looks gorgeous from space': Artemis II shares stunning images of Earth
content: Nasa's Artemis II mission has released stunning first photographs of Earth as the crew journeys toward the moon. Captured by Commander Reid Wiseman, these images showcase our planet's beauty from the Orion spacecraft. This mission, a crucial step for future deep-space exploration and lunar missions, tests systems as the astronauts prepare for their lunar flyby and return to Earth.

title: Ranbir Kapoor reveals ‘Ramayana’ is a six-hour saga; Part 2 shoot halfway done
content: Ranbir Kapoor unveiled exciting details about Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana', a two-part epic set to release this Diwali.  Kapoor described the film as India's 'Lord of the Rings,' boasting six hours of grand visuals, action, and emotional depth.  He confirmed filming for Part 2 is halfway complete, promising an even more thrilling experience for audiences.

title: Too much screen time in kids may look like autism: Doctors explain why
content: Excessive screen exposure in young children is leading to autism-like behaviors, a phenomenon doctors are calling "virtual autism." This condition, distinct from neurodevelopmental autism, is characterized by developmental slowdown and can be reversed by reducing screen time and increasing real-world interaction. Early intervention focusing on play and communication is crucial for recovery.

title: Tragedy at Peru stadium: 1 killed, dozens injured before derby rally
content: Tragedy struck Alejandro Villanueva Stadium in Lima as an incident during a football rally claimed one life and injured 47. While the cause remains unconfirmed, initial reports of a wall collapse were denied by the club. Hospitals are on high alert as authorities investigate the unfortunate event that marred a pre-derby gathering.

title: Hardik Pandya gears up to bowl full quota at 2027 ODI World Cup
content: Hardik Pandya is actively working to prove his fitness for bowling 10 overs in ODIs, aiming to be a key player for the 2027 World Cup. Despite initial concerns from the BCCI, he has demonstrated his readiness in domestic matches and is undergoing rigorous training to assure selectors of his bowling capabilities.

title: 'It's a war': Iran strikes another US jet near Hormuz; Trump says talks remain unaffected
content: In a dramatic turn of events, Iran has intensified confrontations by bringing down a US A-10 Warthog over the vital Strait of Hormuz, following a prior F-15 incident. President Trump labeled the escalating scenario as 'war,' although he reassured that it would not alter the course of negotiations in progress.

title: Shot 5 times inside SUV, man drives 25 km to hospital in Ghaziabad
content: A 35-year-old man, Rahul Chaudhary, was shot five times outside a dhaba in Modinagar. He was on bail for an extortion case. Despite severe injuries, Chaudhary drove himself to a hospital. CCTV footage shows assailants opening fire at his SUV. Police have registered a case and formed teams to apprehend the accused. Investigations are ongoing to identify all involved parties.

title: Heavy rains in Pune halt PMPML operations as 100 buses break down
content: Pune witnessed widespread commuter inconvenience as 97 PMPML buses broke down during sudden heavy showers. Officials admitted unpreparedness, citing unexpected intensity and a focus on summer maintenance. Passengers faced significant hardship with stalled buses and long waits for alternative transport, raising concerns about the transport body's monsoon readiness.

title: 'Anxiety among minorities': Shashi Tharoor questions FCRA Ammendment Bill
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor expressed skepticism regarding reports of the Centre withdrawing the FCRA Amendment Bill, warning of its potential reintroduction. He stated the opposition will remain vigilant to prevent amendments causing anxiety among religious minorities. Opposition MPs had previously protested the bill, citing concerns for NGOs and minority groups

title: Candidates Chess: R Praggnanandhaa wastes his best chance to score win
content: India's R Praggnanandhaa missed a prime chance for victory at the Candidates Tournament, settling for a draw against Andrey Esipenko. Meanwhile, Vaishali Rameshbabu faced her first defeat against Zhu Jiner in the Women's Candidates, while Divya Deshmukh secured a hard-fought draw. The tournament's early stages show a strong lead by Javokhir Sindarov.

title: Jalen Hurts linked to 4 trade spots amid Philadelphia Eagles offensive tensions
content: Speculation is mounting over Jalen Hurts’ future with his parent franchise Philadelphia Eagles. The reasons could be reported offensive friction and his resistance to scheme changes. Analysts suggest potential trade destinations including the Pittsburgh Steelers, Miami Dolphins, New York Jets, and Cleveland Browns, each presenting unique scenarios for the star quarterback.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry join $150b mogul in heartwarming move
content: Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry have joined Warren Buffett in reviving his iconic charity lunch through a new auction titled “A Seat At The Table.” The initiative blends Buffett’s legacy in philanthropy with the Currys’ growing influence beyond basketball. With proceeds benefiting the Glide Foundation and Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation, the event aims to create meaningful change in underserved communities. Scheduled for June 24 in Omaha, the auction reflects a powerful collaboration focused on impact, purpose, and long-term community support.

title: John Cena & The Undertaker reunion set for WrestleMania 42
content: WrestleMania 42 in Las Vegas will feature a major reunion as WWE legends The Undertaker and John Cena share the stage. Cena, who is also the official host for the two-night event, will join The Undertaker for a live, unscripted 'Six Feet Under' recording at WWE World. This highly anticipated fan event promises candid conversations between two iconic figures.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Chicago Bears eye $120M Miami Dolphins star to boost offence
content: NFL trade rumours picked up their pace again as the Chicago Bears are reportedly emerging as a serious contender to acquire veteran wide receiver Tyreek Hill. This potential move follows the team's trade of DJ Moore, creating a void in their receiving corps. Adding Hill's proven deep-threat ability could significantly boost Chicago's offence and its immediate competitiveness. For now, nothing is official. No deal has been agreed. Still, the direction is clear. The Chicago Bears want to boost their offense, and this move could play a big role in that plan if it becomes reality.

title: Middle East continues to boil: Iran rejects US 48-hour ceasefire proposal
content: Iran has reportedly rejected a US proposal for a 48-hour ceasefire, with an unnamed source citing the offer was delivered via an intermediary. Tehran insists on a guaranteed, permanent end to hostilities and has dismissed claims by US President Trump about seeking a temporary truce.

title: Assam horror: Father beheads 6-year-old girl on moving train
content: In a shocking turn of events on the Dekargaon–Murkongselek Express in Assam's Lakhimpur district, a father has now been accused of committing an unimaginable act—his six-year-old daughter Sumi was allegedly beheaded with a machete. Fellow passengers reported feeling uneasy about the father's erratic behavior before this tragic and horrific incident unfolded.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: 'Annamalai’s absence may affect mobilisation’
content: Former BJP state president K Annamalai has been summoned to meet Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Chennai after not being included in the party's candidate list for Tamil Nadu. While the party states he chose to campaign statewide, his absence from the ballot sparks debate within BJP circles regarding its impact on voter mobilization and alliance dynamics.

title: ‘Ramayana’: Ranbir Kapoor confirms his double role — watch
content: Ranbir Kapoor will portray Lord Ram and Lord Parshuram in Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana', confirming a double role. Kapoor shared his approach to differentiating the divine characters, emphasizing spirituality and emotional depth. He acknowledged the immense responsibility of embodying such revered figures, stating his portrayal stems from his personal faith and beliefs as an Indian actor.

title: How a ‘water request’ turned into horror: Govt employee raped at knifepoint
content: In a shocking incident in Macherla, Andhra Pradesh, a 32-year-old woman employed by the government experienced a horrific attack in her home. An unidentified man broke in, brandishing a knife, and committed a violent act against her, using an iron rod in a deeply disturbing manner.

title: Won’t be cowed, says Raghav, AAP brass says he indulged in ‘soft PR’
content: After Raghav Chadha's ousting as deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha, AAP leaders launched an intense critique, branding him as overly focused on superficial publicity while neglecting critical party ideologies. His peers challenged his muted stance on urgent political affairs and his conspicuous lack of presence at protests, suggesting a possible intimidation by PM Modi.

title: Not Jasprit Bumrah: MI coach calls this bowler as team's 'X-factor'
content: The 20-year-old spinner came under focus after conceding 51 runs in Mumbai Indians’ opening match against Kolkata Knight Riders, but Jayawardene backed him as a long-term prospect.

title: Kerala’s development decade
content: Despite a structurally discriminatory federal fiscal architecture, it is a journey marked by unprecedented human development gains and economic growth

title: In Nalanda, faith turned fatal
content: On March 31, over 10,000 devotees, mostly women, thronged the Sheetla Mata temple in Nalanda, Bihar. The temple, which normally holds 500-1,000 people, was overwhelmed, triggering a stampede that claimed nine lives. Victims’ families allege that priests let devotees enter through the exit gate after collecting money. Amit Bhelari reports on how poor crowd management and inadequate arrangements worsened the tragedy

title: Residents demand institutions for higher education, better civic amenities in Mailam
content: Agriculture remains the main occupation but with waterbodies filling up only after a good spell of rain, the farming community depends largely on borewells for cultivation

title: Bhupinder Hooda, AJL discharged in Panchkula land allotment case
content: The special court order follows a February 25 ruling by Punjab and Haryana High Court, which set aside a 2021 order that had framed charges against former Haryana Chief Minister and others in the case

title: 'Frustrated' Geno, Staley have fiery sideline dustup
content: UConn's Geno Auriemma ripped into the officiating crew during a live TV interview and then got into a heated argument with Dawn Staley in the final seconds of the Huskies' 62-48 loss to South Carolina in the women's Final Four on Friday night.

title: UCLA grinds out sloppy win, makes 1st title game
content: It wasn't pretty, but UCLA survived 23 turnovers and held off a late surge from Texas to pull out a 51-44 win on Friday night to reach the NCAA title game for the first time in program history.

title: Mavs' Flagg, 19, is youngest to have 50-pt game
content: Mavericks forward Cooper Flagg became the youngest player in NBA history to have a 50-point game, as the rookie scored 51 in Friday's 138-127 home loss to the Magic.

title: Sources: NBA investigating Bucks, Giannis' health
content: The NBA is investigating the Bucks' handling of Giannis Antetokounmpo, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania, after the star forward told reporters Friday that he is being held out despite being healthy enough to play.

title: Doncic set to miss at least rest of regular season
content: Lakers star Luka Doncic has a Grade 2 hamstring strain and will be out at least through the end of the regular season, the team announced Friday.

title: 2 HCs push back after LeBron's Memphis remark
content: A pair of NBA head coaches defended Memphis and its culture Friday following criticism by Lakers star LeBron James that the Grizzlies should just relocate.

title: 'Difference-maker': Griffin dazzles in Pirates debut
content: Pittsburgh Pirates shortstop Konnor Griffin made his major league debut and hit an RBI double as Pittsburgh beat the Baltimore Orioles 5-4 in its home opener at PNC Park.

title: Fire make Carleton top WNBA expansion draft pick
content: Unrestricted free agent Bridget Carleton was selected by the Portland Fire as the No. 1 pick in the 2026 WNBA expansion draft Friday.

title: 👀 Latest NBA buzz after Luka's injury
content: Here's what league insiders are talking about behind the scenes, including when Doncic might be to return from his latest injury.

title: The incredibly rare thing you probably missed during the Cubs' 2016 curse-breaking World Series win
content: While the Cubs were making history in Cleveland 10 years ago, one unique accomplishment flew well under the radar.

title: Final NBA MVP (and ROY) straw poll: Here's who voters are picking to win
content: Can Wemby's late push unseat SGA? Tim Bontemps polls 100 NBA insiders for the latest MVP snapshot. Plus, a look at the race for top rookie.

title: Latest NFL draft buzz: What we're hearing on Jermod McCoy's pro day, the QB3 debate
content: How did the Tennessee CB perform at his pro day? Who is the third-best QB in this class? Our draft experts weigh in with new intel three weeks out.

title: Safety 'savant' Caleb Downs to test NFL's thirst for his position
content: Since 2000, only eight safeties have been drafted in the top 10. Downs could challenge conventional wisdom.

title: 'We want to pinch ourselves': Dusty May's full-circle return to the Final Four
content: Three years after helming FAU's Cinderella run to the national semifinals, May returns with Michigan.

title: Your men's Final Four guide: Illinois-UConn, Arizona-Michigan (with predictions!)
content: We run through every team's keys to advancing to the championship game -- and their potential fatal flaws.

title: NHL playoff watch: The Flyers' path to the postseason
content: Philly needs a win Friday -- and some help, too. Plus, full updated playoff projections after a 14-game night.

title: Who are the 39 best men's U21 players in the world?
content: For the eighth straight year, ESPN brings you our list of the game's next young stars aged 21 or under. Who is good enough to make it?

title: Weekend predictions: Man City or Liverpool in FA Cup? Can Atlético play spoiler vs. Barça?
content: From a big-club clash in England to a potentially decisive LaLiga matchup, another wild weekend of action awaits as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

title: Premier League without set-piece goals: Arsenal still first?
content: We crunched the numbers on how the standings would look like without goals from corners, free kicks and more.

title: Scouts debate the top of the NBA draft class: Peterson or Dybantsa at No. 1?
content: Fit over talent? Versatility over production? Scouts and execs break down the conversations happening.

title: UConn on brink of dual title appearance brings 2014 to mind
content: UConn men's and women's basketball are both in the Final Four. Could the school make its first dual national championship appearance since 2014? The world was quite different back then.

title: Tracking fifth-year options for 2023 first-rounders: Who will return in 2027?
content: The deadline for teams to pick up the fifth-year option on rookie contracts is May 1. Which players have had their options picked up?

title: Men's turnament picks: How to bet Saturday's national semifinal games
content: Will the Illini get revenge for the loss to UConn back in November? Is the total too high in the late game?

title: Alvarez a top-10 bat? Ohtani wins Cy Young? Perdomo for real? Don't be surprised
